scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE District Court / Civil Court
AT Bengaluru

O.S. No. __________ / 20____

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 113 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff states that the Defendant is known to the Plaintiff and had approached the Plaintiff requesting financial assistance by way of a hand loan. Acting on the request and based on trust, the Plaintiff agreed to advance the sum to the Defendant.

6. On 15.03.2024, the Plaintiff advanced a sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs only) to the Defendant via NEFT bank transfer. The transaction is evidenced by the bank record bearing UTR No. AXIB20240315123456, which is annexed herewith as Annexure A.

7. Although no written loan agreement was executed at the time of advancement, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount and the liability to repay the same through digital communications on WhatsApp. Copies of the relevant WhatsApp messages are annexed herewith as Annexure B.

8. The loan was advanced on the understanding that the Defendant would repay the amount on demand or within a reasonable time, and the parties orally agreed that the repayment was due by 20.06.2024.

BREACH AND PARTICULARS OF DEFAULT

9. The Defendant failed to repay the loan amount on the agreed date of 20.06.2024, thereby committing a breach of the oral agreement and causing financial hardship to the Plaintiff.

10. Despite repeated oral demands made upon the Defendant to return the principal amount, the Defendant neglected and refused to repay the same. Consequently, the Plaintiff issued a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 calling upon the Defendant to repay the dues, which is annexed herewith as Annexure C.

11. Despite receipt of the said legal notice, the Defendant has failed to comply with the demand or repay any portion of the loan amount, leaving the Plaintiff with no alternative but to institute the present suit.

DAMAGES

12. The Defendant is legally liable to pay the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- which remains due and outstanding to date. The Plaintiff is entitled to recover the entire principal amount as a debt due from the Defendant.

13. Further damages are due by way of interest at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default i.e., 20.06.2024 until the date of realisation. The Plaintiff claims this interest as compensation for the wrongful retention of the Plaintiff's money by the Defendant.

LEGAL BASIS

14. The present cause is governed by Indian Contract Act, 1872, including Section 73.

15. The present cause is governed by Code of Civil Procedure, 1908, including Section 9, Section 20, Section 34.

CAUSE OF ACTION

16. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to the Plaintiff's right to institute the present suit.

17. The cause of action further arose when the Defendant, despite receipt of the legal notice dated {{NOTICE_DATE}}, failed and refused to comply with the Plaintiff's demand.

18. The cause of action is not a continuing cause of action. The breach consisted of a single event. However, the Plaintiff's right to damages subsists.

VALUATION AND COURT FEE

19. The suit is valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only) for the purposes of jurisdiction and court fee. Court fee of Rs. {{COURT_FEE_AMOUNT}}/- has been paid on this plaint under the provisions of the Court Fees Act, 1870 as applicable in the State of Karnataka. The plaint is properly stamped.

INTEREST

20. The Plaintiff claims interest on the
